content.full_text:
CITY OF
TORRANCE

OFFICE OF
THE CITY ATTORNEY
KENNETH L. NELSON

August 20, 1992

Scott Hallagrin, General Counsel
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Hallagrin:

As City Attorney for the City of Torrance and Counsel to
the Redevelopment Agency, this letter shall serve as a request
pursuant to 2 California Administrative Code Section 18329 for
advice on the facts contained herein. We have briefly
discussed this matter with John Wallace of your staff.

This request is made concerning Don Lee, Council Member
and Board Member of the Redevelopment Agency of the City of
Torrance. Don Lee was elected to the City Council in March
1992. From May 1988 to March 1992, Don Lee served as a member
of the Planning Commission.

Councilman Lee owns and operates an insurance agency.
This business is located within the City's Downtown
Redevelopment Project. Councilman Lee leases the property
where his business is located. He entered into the four-year
lease in June of 1990. The lease includes a four-year renewal
option. The plan for this project was adopted in 1979. The
project covers eighty-eight (88) acres. Approximately
ninety-five (95) percent of the project is commercial and
approximately five (5) percent is residential. There are two
hundred to three hundred businesses within the project.
Gascon-Mar is the developer for the entire project.

Gascon-Mar paid one months' rent to each of the seven
tenants on the first floor of the building where Councilman Lee
leases his business property. This amounted to a payment of
six hundred and twelve dollars and seventy cents ($612.70) to
the Councilman's insurance agency. We have heard that tenants
in other buildings may have received payment for their
inconvenience from payments by Gascon-Mar to their landlords.

3031 Torrance Boulevard • Torrance, California 90503 • Telephone 310/618-5810 • Facsimile 310/618-5813

I can see this appears to be a mostly blank or very faded document image. The image shows what looks like a folded or multi-page document with horizontal fold lines visible, but I cannot make out any legible text content to transcribe.

If there is text present in this document, it is too faint or unclear for me to read reliably. If you have a clearer version of this document, I would be happy to transcribe it accurately.

Councilman Lee has requested advice regarding his
participation in future decisions of the City Council and the
Redevelopment Agency on the Downtown Redevelopment Project.
Currently he has not been voting on the project.

Councilman Lee has a maximum of fifteen (15) City
employees as clients of his insurance business. The maximum
commission received from one City employee in one year was
four hundred dollars ($400). Councilman Lee has requested
advice regarding his participation in decisions such as
memorandums of understanding with employee groups representing
his clients.

Please feel free to contact me if any additional
information is necessary for your response.

Very truly yours,

[illegible] [illegible]
Kenneth L. Nelson
City Attorney

EGC/wm/271


2

The image appears to be a blank or nearly blank page with very faint text or markings that are not legible enough to transcribe with confidence. The document shows mostly white/light gray space with some subtle texture or artifacts from scanning, but no clearly readable text content is visible.
